data.title: IQVIA sees 2025 revenue growth of 4%-7% amid strong demand recovery
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: IQVIA Holdings Inc. (NYSE:IQV) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Ari Bousbib highlighted that IQVIA achieved solid revenue growth of 5.5% at constant currency for the full year, excluding COVID-related revenues. Adjusted diluted EPS increased by over 9%, while free cash flow grew 41% year-over-year to $2.1 billion.
  • Bousbib attributed these results to strong operational efforts despite macroeconomic challenges, including the Inflation Reduction Act's impact and geopolitical uncertainties. He emphasized that IQVIA outperformed in its sector despite an elevated level of cancellations in R&D Solutions (R&DS).
  • Bousbib stated, "Revenue in Q4 exceeded the high end of guidance, growing 4.5% at constant currency excluding COVID-related work. Adjusted diluted EPS grew nearly 10% year-over-year, and the quarter marked a record for free cash flow."
  • CFO Ron Bruehlman reported Q4 revenue of $3.958 billion, an increase of 3% at constant currency, driven by strong performance in Technology & Analytics Solutions (TAS), which grew 9.5% constant currency. He noted adjusted diluted EPS of $3.12, up 9.9% year-over-year.

Outlook

  • The company reaffirmed its 2025 guidance, projecting revenue growth of 4%-7% at constant currency, excluding COVID-related revenues. Adjusted EBITDA margin expansion is anticipated at up to 20 basis points, with adjusted diluted EPS growth expected between 5%-9%.
  • For Q1 2025, IQVIA expects revenue between $3.74 billion and $3.79 billion, adjusted EBITDA between $870 million and $890 million, and adjusted EPS between $2.60 and $2.70.
  • Bousbib noted that TAS demand continued to recover as anticipated, while R&DS may experience another quarter or two of volatility due to ongoing portfolio reprioritizations at large pharma clients.

Financial Results

  • Q4 adjusted EBITDA increased by 3.1%, with margins expanding by 20 basis points year-over-year. Full-year adjusted EBITDA reached $3.684 billion, a 3.2% year-over-year increase.
  • Free cash flow in Q4 totaled $721 million, setting a quarterly record. Full-year free cash flow reached $2.114 billion, up 41% year-over-year.
  • The R&DS segment recorded Q4 revenue of $2.123 billion, a 1% increase at constant currency when excluding COVID-related revenues. TAS revenue for the quarter stood at $1.658 billion, growing 9.5% at constant currency.
  • The company repurchased $1.15 billion of shares during Q4, bringing the full-year total to $1.35 billion. The Board authorized an additional $2 billion for share repurchases, increasing the total authorization to $3 billion.

Q&A

  • Shlomo Rosenbaum, Stifel, asked about ongoing volatility in the R&DS environment and large trial delays. CEO Ari Bousbib explained that most reprioritizations have been completed, with approximately 75% of portfolio adjustments resolved. He also confirmed that delays in two mega trials, due to client logistics, remain on track to resume in late 2025.
  • Elizabeth Anderson, Evercore ISI, queried the biotech funding environment and drivers for real-world evidence growth. Bousbib noted that biotech funding exceeded $100 billion in 2024, with mid-single-digit increases in RFP flow observed. He highlighted accelerated adoption of real-world solutions, driven by regulatory submissions and efficacy demonstrations.
  • David Windley, Jefferies, inquired about cost management amid pricing pressures and trial delays. Bousbib acknowledged elevated costs due to stranded resources from delayed trials but emphasized operational discipline, including AI-driven efficiencies, to mitigate headwinds.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed concerns over continued cancellations and pricing pressures but generally maintained a balanced tone. Questions emphasized operational resilience and long-term demand recovery.
  • Management maintained a confident stance during prepared remarks, with Bousbib emphasizing IQVIA’s strong positioning despite industry challenges. During Q&A, he acknowledged short-term uncertainties while reiterating optimism for 2025 growth.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst sentiment appeared cautiously optimistic, reflecting greater clarity on reprioritizations and demand recovery. Management’s tone remained consistent, emphasizing strategic wins and innovation.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q4 revenue growth decelerated to 3% constant currency compared to Q3’s 4.2%, reflecting the impact of elevated cancellations and delayed trials in R&DS.
  • Adjusted EPS growth slowed from 14.1% in Q3 to 9.9% in Q4, largely due to stranded costs associated with delayed trials. However, TAS growth improved sequentially, supported by double-digit gains in real-world solutions.
  • Management reiterated confidence in 2025 growth projections, contrasting with the slightly more cautious tone adopted in Q3 regarding near-term volatility.

Risks and Concerns

  • Elevated cancellations in R&DS, up nearly 50% year-over-year, remain a concern. Management acknowledged potential volatility in the next one to two quarters.
  • Pricing pressures, particularly in competitive CRO segments, were highlighted as a persistent challenge.
  • Delays in two large trials have created short-term cost headwinds, though management assured that these would resume in 2025.

Final Takeaway

IQVIA closed 2024 with robust financial performance despite macroeconomic challenges and elevated cancellations in its R&DS segment. Management reaffirmed 2025 growth guidance, citing strong demand recovery in TAS and biotechnology funding as key drivers. While short-term volatility persists, the company remains confident in its ability to navigate challenges and sustain long-term growth. Share repurchases and innovation in AI-driven solutions signal continued shareholder and operational focus.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Eldorado Gold hikes Skouries capital cost estimate to more than $1B, extends timeline
data.description:
Eldorado Gold (NYSE:EGO) -10.7% in Thursday's trading after boosting the capital cost estimate and extending the timeline for its Skouries copper-gold project in Greece, citing a tight labor market in Greece that continues to limit the availability of key construction personnel.

The miner said it now expects first production at Skouries in Q1 2026, with commercial production expected by mid-year, and revised its project capital cost estimate by $143M, or 15.5% over prior capital cost estimates, to a total of ~$1.06B.

Eldorado (NYSE:EGO) said it achieved a daily on-site total of ~1,150 workers at Skouries in late January, while it has targeted a workforce of 1,300 on site through the peak of construction activities.

The company also said it has accelerated the purchase of higher capacity mobile mining equipment, which originally was expected to be bought after commercial production, resulting in an additional $154M in pre-commercial production operational capital.

At year-end 2024, Eldorado (EGO) incurred $512M of capital expenditures at Skouries, with $705M of remaining expenditures expected to achieve commercial production.

data.title: Zimmer Biomet projects 3%-5% revenue growth for 2025 with over 50 product launches in 36 months
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Zimmer Biomet (NYSE:ZBH) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Ivan Tornos highlighted that Zimmer Biomet achieved nearly 5% constant currency sales growth in Q4 2024, marking the 12th consecutive quarter of mid-single-digit or better growth. The performance was driven by mid-single-digit growth in hips and knees as well as upper single-digit growth in S.E.T.
  • The company maintained its full-year 2024 financial commitments, delivering approximately $8 in adjusted EPS and $1.5 billion in free cash flow.
  • CEO Tornos emphasized the upcoming integration of Paragon 28, a leader in the $5 billion foot and ankle market, which is expected to close in the first half of 2025. He noted that this acquisition will strengthen Zimmer Biomet's portfolio in foot and ankle procedures and create cross-selling opportunities.
  • CFO Suketu Upadhyay reported Q4 2024 revenue of $2.023 billion, a 4.9% increase on a constant currency basis. Adjusted EPS was $2.31, demonstrating growth from the prior year. He highlighted strong performance in global S.E.T., which grew 8.4%.
  • CFO Upadhyay also noted that free cash flow for 2024 reached $1.055 billion, with days on hand inventory reduced by nearly 50 days.

Outlook

  • Zimmer Biomet provided 2025 guidance of constant currency revenue growth between 3%-5% and adjusted EPS in the range of $8.15 to $8.35. These projections exclude the impact of the Paragon 28 acquisition.
  • Management expects a stronger revenue and margin profile in the second half of 2025, driven by new product ramp-ups, favorable year-over-year comparisons, and operational improvements.
  • CEO Tornos stated that the company plans to launch over 50 new products in the next 36 months, with several categorized as disruptive innovations, particularly in hips, knees, and robotics.

Financial Results

  • Adjusted EPS for Q4 2024 came in at $2.31 vs. analysts' estimate of $2.29. Reported revenue of $2.023 billion slightly surpassed the analysts' estimate of $2.015 billion.
  • Global S.E.T. growth was a standout at 8.4%, with notable contributions from CMFT, sports medicine, and upper extremities. This marks the fifth consecutive quarter of mid-single-digit or better growth in the segment.
  • Global knees grew 5.6%, with the U.S. segment up 3.9%, supported by increased penetration of the Persona portfolio and ROSA Robotics. Hips grew 4%, bolstered by the initial rollout of the Z1 Triple Tapered Stem.
  • Adjusted gross margin was reported at 71.3%, while adjusted operating margin expanded by 50 basis points year-over-year to 30.8%.

Q&A

  • Robbie Marcus, JPMorgan: Questioned the guidance philosophy, noting its conservative nature. CEO Tornos clarified that the 2025 range is deemed appropriate and highlighted drivers such as new product launches and ASC market investments as key factors to achieving the upper end.
  • Patrick Wood, Morgan Stanley: Inquired about the Paragon 28 integration strategy. CEO Tornos emphasized that the acquisition would be run in isolation initially to minimize disruption, with revenue synergies expected from ASC market opportunities and international expansion.
  • Steven Lichtman, Oppenheimer: Asked about 2025 sales and margin cadence. CFO Upadhyay projected stronger performance in the second half, driven by new product contributions and operational efficiencies.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed a cautiously optimistic sentiment, particularly regarding the 2025 guidance and the Paragon 28 acquisition. Questions focused on conservatism in guidance and execution risks.
  • Management maintained a confident tone during prepared remarks, emphasizing innovation, operational improvements, and the potential of new product launches. However, in the Q&A, there was a nuanced tone, with careful attention to mitigating potential integration risks for Paragon 28.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management exhibited increased confidence in resolving ERP challenges and leveraging the product pipeline for growth.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue growth in Q4 2024 was slightly higher than Q3, supported by robust S.E.T. performance and easing ERP challenges.
  • 2025 guidance reflects a shift in focus towards new product ramp-ups and operational improvements, compared to the previous quarter’s emphasis on resolving ERP disruptions.
  • Analysts' focus shifted from ERP recovery to the impact of new product launches and the Paragon 28 acquisition.

Risks and Concerns

  • Potential challenges in integrating Paragon 28 were noted, particularly in managing the independent salesforce and maintaining operational continuity. CEO Tornos reassured minimal disruption.
  • Macroeconomic factors, including FX headwinds, were highlighted as potential risks to margins and earnings growth in 2025.
  • Analysts raised questions about the sustainability of positive pricing trends and the ability to ramp up new product launches effectively.

Final Takeaway

Zimmer Biomet delivered solid Q4 2024 results, meeting or exceeding most expectations. The company's focus on innovation, with over 50 planned product launches, and its acquisition of Paragon 28 are expected to drive growth in 2025 and beyond. Management’s guidance for 2025 reflects confidence in both revenue growth and operational improvements, although risks related to integration and macroeconomic headwinds remain areas to watch.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Stocks mixed as Wall Street sifts through earnings, labor market data
data.description:
U.S. stocks were largely muted Thursday, as investors sorted through a new wave of earnings reports and labor market updates, including a jump in job cuts, ahead of Friday's monthly U.S. payrolls report.

The S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) rising would mark their third consecutive advances. Market sentiment took a hit early after Qualcomm (QCOM) and Ford (F) both fell more than 5% after their reports. Along with January jobs data in sight, Amazon (AMZN) is expected late Thursday to report growth in Q4 earnings and sales.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SPX) +0.1%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was flat. The blue-chip Dow (DJI) -0.4%, with Honeywell (HON) -6% after its FY25 outlook missed expectations.

The Financials sector led six other groups higher on the benchmark (SP500). Energy fell -1.3%. Consumer names were the index's strongest individual performers. Coach parent Tapestry (TPR) +12%, after raising FY2025 guidance. Ralph Lauren (RL) +12%, and Philip Morris (PM) +9.3% following quarterly results.

Labor-market reports were also under scrutiny. The number of job cuts announced by U.S. employers surged 28% to 49,795 in January from December, according to Challenger Gray & Christmas data. But they fell 40% from January 2024.

Initial jobless claims rose more than expected in the past week. Meanwhile, Q4 U.S. nonfarm labor costs rose by a less-than-expected 3.0% Q/Q, and nonfarm productivity increased 1.2%, lagging the +1.8% consensus.

On Thursday, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was unchanged at 4.43%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was up nearly 2 basis points to 4.21%.

"Tomorrow’s NFP for January is likely to be a big market mover," Bob Lang, founder and chief options analyst at Explosive Options, said.

Despite Wednesday's stronger-than-expected ADP private hiring report, January nonfarm payrolls may rise 145K vs. 256K in December, "with downside risk possible from the effects of the Los Angeles fires," CFRA's Chief Investment Strategist Sam Stovall, said, citing Action Economics figures.

Stocks on Wednesday rose, overcoming pressure brought on in part by a slump in shares of Alphabet (GOOG)(GOOGL).

"It was important to see the bulls come back and deliver a win. Markets have been teetering of late right near the all-time highs and just cannot seem to find buyers to cross the finish line," Lang said.

"No question we believe the time is coming for new highs to be achieved, specifically on the SPX 500, Industrials and Nasdaq," he said.

data.title: Hillenbrand projects $685M-$705M Q2 revenue with focus on deleveraging and portfolio transformation
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Hillenbrand, Inc. (NYSE:HI) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Kimberly Ryan highlighted the sale of a 51% stake in the Milacron injection molding and extrusion business to Bain Capital for $287 million, retaining a 49% stake. This move aligns with Hillenbrand's strategy to focus on higher-margin, less-cyclical businesses.
  • Consolidated Q1 revenue was $707 million, reflecting a 9% decrease year-over-year, attributed to lower volume and starting backlog. Adjusted EPS of $0.56 also declined 19% but was in line with expectations.
  • Advanced Process Solutions (APS) segment achieved record aftermarket orders, particularly in Food, Health, and Nutrition (FHN). The integration of recently acquired assets is progressing ahead of schedule, with high teens margins achieved in this segment.
  • CFO Robert VanHimbergen reported adjusted EBITDA of $97 million, a 15% decline due to lower volume and cost inflation, partially offset by favorable pricing and synergies.

Outlook

  • Hillenbrand anticipates Q2 revenue in the range of $685 million to $705 million and adjusted EPS between $0.53 and $0.58.
  • Full-year guidance has been updated to reflect the Milacron transaction, projecting revenue between $2.63 billion and $2.8 billion, adjusted EBITDA of $411 million to $447 million, and adjusted EPS of $2.45 to $2.80.
  • Management remains cautious about macroeconomic uncertainties, including interest rates and inflation, but expects modest sequential improvement in orders starting in Q2.

Financial Results

  • APS revenue was $511 million, down 10% year-over-year, with adjusted EBITDA margins at 16.2%. Despite headwinds, the segment benefited from favorable pricing and cost synergies.
  • Molding Technology Solutions (MTS) revenue decreased 5% to $196 million, with adjusted EBITDA margins at 14%. Management noted ongoing softness in North America and Europe but observed stability in Asia and growth in India.
  • Consolidated adjusted EBITDA margins for Q1 were 13.7%, a decline of 110 basis points year-over-year, driven by lower volume.
  • Net debt stood at $1.7 billion, with a net debt-to-EBITDA ratio of 3.4x. All proceeds from the Milacron sale will be used for debt reduction, targeting a leverage ratio in the low 3s by fiscal year-end.

Q&A

  • Daniel Moore, CJS Securities: Asked about barriers to capital equipment orders in APS. CEO Ryan cited geopolitical instability, interest rates, and tariffs as key factors delaying customer decisions.
  • Mitchell Moore, KeyBanc Capital Markets: Inquired about the decision to retain a minority stake in Milacron. CEO Ryan explained that this structure allows Hillenbrand to focus on its core business while maintaining potential future returns.
  • John Franzreb, Sidoti & Company: Asked about geographic demand in India and China. CEO Ryan noted strong growth in India and stabilization in China, supported by local-for-local supply chain strategies.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism but raised concerns about delayed capital investment decisions and macroeconomic uncertainties. Questions reflected skepticism about the timeline for recovery in key markets.
  • Management's tone was confident during prepared remarks, emphasizing strategic initiatives and resilience in the APS segment. However, responses in the Q&A were more measured, acknowledging persistent challenges.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management maintained a similar level of confidence but with increased focus on mitigating near-term risks.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q1 results showed a decline in revenue and EPS compared to Q4 2024, which had benefited from stronger aftermarket performance and higher backlog.
  • Guidance language reflected increased caution due to the Milacron transaction and ongoing macroeconomic pressures.
  • Analysts’ focus shifted from broad market recovery to the strategic implications of the Milacron sale and its impact on Hillenbrand’s financial position.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted continued macroeconomic uncertainty, including inflation, interest rates, and geopolitical tensions, as key factors affecting customer investment decisions.
  • Analysts raised questions about price pressure in the MTS segment and the timeline for recovery in key end markets such as automotive and packaging.
  • Hillenbrand’s reliance on capital equipment orders and project timing remains a potential risk in the near term.

Final Takeaway

Hillenbrand’s Q1 2025 earnings call underscored its ongoing transformation toward a higher-margin and less-cyclical business model, highlighted by the majority sale of its Milacron business. Despite revenue and EPS declines, results were in line with expectations. Management remains focused on debt reduction and leveraging its APS segment’s growth potential, particularly in aftermarket and FHN markets. However, macroeconomic uncertainties and delayed capital investments continue to pose challenges, prompting cautious guidance for the upcoming quarters.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Aflac signals new cancer insurance launch and Tsumitasu expansion for 2025 growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Aflac Incorporated (NYSE:AFL) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Dan Amos highlighted strong performance for the year, with net earnings per diluted share rising 23.8% and adjusted earnings per diluted share increasing 15.7%. Aflac Japan accounted for over 70% of pre-tax adjusted earnings and achieved a record 36% pretax profit margin for the year. Persistency in Japan remained solid at 93.4%, while U.S. persistency improved by 70 basis points to 79.3%.

  • Amos emphasized the success of the Tsumitasu product in Japan, which targets younger customers for asset formation and nursing care needs. The product has driven a 5.6% year-over-year sales increase in Japan, including a 9% sales rise in Q4.

  • Aflac plans to launch a new cancer insurance product in Japan between March and April 2025. This product will include enhanced coverage before, during, and after cancer treatment, along with a new plan tailored for children.

  • CFO Max Broden stated that adjusted earnings per diluted share for Q4 rose 24.8% year-over-year to $1.56, with a $0.01 negative impact from FX. He noted that variable investment income exceeded long-term return expectations, and adjusted book value per share increased 3.2%.

Outlook

  • Broden projected Japan’s benefit ratio for 2025 to be at the higher end of the 64% to 66% range and its expense ratio at the lower end of 20% to 23%. The Japan pretax margin is expected to be at the lower end of the 30% to 33% range for 2025.

  • In the U.S., the benefit ratio is forecasted to be at the lower end of the 48% to 52% range, while the pretax profit margin is anticipated to reach the upper end of the 17% to 20% range.

Financial Results

  • Aflac Japan reported a 5.4% decline in net earned premiums for Q4 due to internal cancer reinsurance transactions and the impact of paid-up policies. The pretax margin for Japan in Q4 was 31.6%, an improvement of 120 basis points year-over-year.

  • In the U.S., net earned premiums increased 2.7% for the quarter, and the pretax margin stood at 19.7%. Adjusted net investment income in the U.S. was up 0.9% for the quarter.

  • The company highlighted a $40 million increase in CECL reserves for its commercial real estate portfolio, reflecting continued challenges in the market.

Q&A

  • Joel Hurwitz, Dowling & Partners, asked Virgil Miller about the competitive environment impacting U.S. sales. Miller noted that stricter underwriting discipline and challenges in recovering from a dental system implementation issue contributed to softer sales in Q4 but emphasized positive trends in persistency and earned premiums.

  • Jimmy Bhullar, J.P. Morgan, inquired about Japan’s sales momentum. Koichiro Yoshizumi explained that the Tsumitasu product would continue to generate solid results, supported by marketing and agency efforts.

  • Wes Carmichael, Autonomous Research, questioned remeasurement gains trends. Broden clarified that while the company has seen favorable remeasurement gains, rising claims in specific products like accident and hospital policies may temper future gains.

  • Josh Shanker, Bank of America, asked about the elevated expense ratio in the U.S. Miller stated that scaling new initiatives, such as the PLAD platform and consumer markets, weighed on the ratio but expects improvement in 2025.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism but raised concerns over competitive pressures in both the U.S. and Japan. Questions frequently sought clarity on sales momentum, product launches, and macroeconomic impacts.

  • Management maintained a confident tone during prepared remarks, highlighting strategic initiatives such as the new cancer product launch and disciplined underwriting.

  • Compared to the prior quarter, the tone from analysts reflected increased scrutiny on execution risks, particularly in U.S. sales recovery and Japan’s product mix strategy.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Aflac's Q4 results showed continued strength in Japan's pretax margin, driven by persistency and cost management, while U.S. performance faced challenges in sales despite improvements in persistency.

  • Management's tone remained consistent, emphasizing long-term growth potential in Japan and disciplined expense management in the U.S.

  • The focus on Tsumitasu and the upcoming cancer insurance launch marked a strategic shift in Japan, whereas the prior quarter highlighted the stabilization of U.S. operations.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged challenges in Japan’s declining in-force policies and U.S. dental platform recovery.

  • The commercial real estate portfolio remains under pressure, with property values at distressed levels and increased CECL reserves.

  • Analysts questioned the sustainability of Tsumitasu-driven growth in Japan and competitive pressures in the voluntary benefits market in the U.S.

Final Takeaway

Aflac's Q4 2024 earnings call underscored its focus on leveraging innovative products like Tsumitasu in Japan and disciplined underwriting in the U.S. While Japan remains a strong profit center, strategic launches such as the new cancer insurance aim to sustain growth. In the U.S., management acknowledged challenges in sales but emphasized long-term profitability driven by improved persistency and scaling new initiatives. Capital deployment remained robust, with a focus on shareholder returns and strategic investments.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Li Auto shares trade higher as Macquarie upgrades to ‘Outperform’
data.description:
Li Auto (NASDAQ:LI) shares were up more than 6.7% in Thursday trade after investment group Macquarie upgraded the stock from ‘neutral’ to ‘outperform’.

Electric vehicle stocks have been trading higher this week on renewed hope that a damaging trade between the U.S. and China can be avoided. Investors are showing some limited confidence that the tariff battle will wind down quickly.

After Trump’s tariffs, China retaliated with a levy of limited tariffs on imports of multiple U.S. products, including LNG, coal, and more. So far, the electric vehicle supply chain was not directly targeted.

Li Auto said earlier this month that it delivered 29,927 vehicles in January 2025, bringing its cumulative deliveries to 1,163,799 vehicles.

Despite Macquarie’s upgrade, Seeking Alpha’s Quant has a HOLD rating on the stock. However, Wall Street analysts and SA authors are optimistic and have rated the stock a BUY.

Victor Dergunov, Investing Group Leader for The Financial Prophet, believes that substantial growth potential makes Li Auto a strong competitor to Tesla and BYD.

“Despite inherent risks associated with Chinese stocks, Li Auto's solid financials and market position suggest a significant upside in the coming years,” Dergunov added.

data.title: US Steel gains on report President Trump will meet with steel firm
data.description:
US Steel (NYSE:X) hit session highs, gaining 4.3%, after a report that President Donald Trump is scheduled to meet with the steel producer today.

Trump is meeting with the leaders of US Steel (NYSE:X) and FedEx (FDX) on Thursday, according a posting on X from an ABC reporter.

The stock gained as investors are likely hoping that US Steel (X) can convince Trump to support the company's planned $14 billion sale to Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY), which was blocked by Former President Joe Biden last month.

The meeting comes as Trump is scheduled to meet with Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba on Friday. Ishiba is expected to highlight that Japan is the biggest investor in the U.S., leading foreign direct investment for the past five years, and discuss how Japanese companies are creating American jobs in the meeting on Friday, according to a Nikkei report on Tuesday.

Earlier Thursday, it was reported that Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY) said its proposed takeover of US Steel (X) matches President Trump's goal of a stronger United States.

data.title: Lyft teams up with Anthropic to improve customer service features
data.description:
Lyft (NASDAQ:LYFT) and AI startup Anthropic unveiled a groundbreaking partnership aimed at transforming the rideshare experience for Lyft's (NASDAQ:LYFT) network of 40 million annual riders and over 1 million drivers.

The collaboration centers on developing innovative AI-powered products that prioritize customer needs while maintaining a human-centric approach, aligning with Anthropic's reputation for creating empathetic AI solutions.

The partnership encompasses three key areas of cooperation. First, the companies will work together to develop and deploy Anthropic-powered solutions across Lyft's business operations. Second, Lyft will gain early access to research testing of new Anthropic offerings, ensuring that future capabilities align with the needs of riders and drivers. Lastly, Anthropic will provide training and education to Lyft's engineering team, preparing them to lead the next wave of innovation in the rideshare industry.

Lyft (LYFT) has already incorporated Anthropic's Claude AI model into its customer care assistant via Amazon Bedrock. The integration was noted to have yielded impressive results, reducing the average customer service resolution time by 87% and handling thousands of requests daily.

Looking down the road, the long-term collaboration aims to enhance the Lyft (LYFT) rideshare experience by leveraging AI to create innovative products that foster human connection and improve efficiency for both riders and drivers.
